title: Loops & Sequences
description: Master loops and sequential programming to create repeating patterns and efficient code.
fullDescription: Learn how to use loops to repeat actions and create efficient programs. This lesson covers repeat blocks, sequences, and pattern creation.

teacherInstructions.setup: Prepare grid paper and repeat-pattern cards so students can first model loops away from the screen.
steps: Show a repetitive task and ask how to shorten instructions (3 min).
Introduce repeat blocks with one simple visual example (5 min).
Have students build a pattern using a loop and test it (8 min).
Challenge students to reduce block count while keeping the same output (6 min).
discussionPrompts: When is a loop better than repeating blocks by hand?
How can we tell if a loop runs too many times?
Where do we see repetition in real life or games?
tips: Use visual counters so students can track loop iterations.
Start with low repetition numbers before scaling complexity.
Ask students to explain loop logic out loud before coding.

teacherGuide: Introduce the concept of repetition and loops. Show how using a repeat block is more efficient than copying the same block multiple times. Have students practice with simple 2-3 iteration loops, then gradually increase complexity. Visual demonstrations are key for understanding.

studentExercises.title: Create a Simple Loop
studentExercises.description: Use a repeat block to draw the same shape 4 times

studentExercises.title: Pattern Creation
studentExercises.description: Create a repeating pattern using loops

studentExercises.title: Nested Loops
studentExercises.description: Use loops within loops to create complex patterns
